Noted Behavior: Standard baseline with default CNN hyperparameters. Agent learned basic paddle movement and began tracking the ball. Stable but unoptimized performance.
Higher learning rate accelerated initial learning but introduced instability. Agent showed erratic paddle movement at times yet scored higher on average due to aggressive Q-value updates.
Very slow learning rate severely hampered the agent. Weights updated too slowly to capture fast ball dynamics within the given training budget.
Near-future-maximizing discount factor. Agent placed high value on long-term rewards but converged slowly; slight improvement over baseline.
Short-sighted but effective strategy. Lower gamma pushed the agent to exploit immediate rewards aggressively; surprisingly competitive performance.
Larger batch provided more stable gradient estimates. Consistent top-tier performance with reduced variance; better generalization per update step.
Very small batch led to noisy updates. Agent survived many steps but scored poorly; high variance in policy updates prevented effective learning.
Slower exploration decay kept the agent exploring longer. Good balance of exploration and exploitation; marginally better than baseline.
MLP policy lacked spatial feature extraction capability essential for image-based Atari tasks. Scored ~51% less than CNN baseline confirming CNNs superiority for visual RL.
Best configuration. Balanced learning rate with moderate gamma and large batch produced optimal reward. Very low epsilon_end ensured near-greedy exploitation at convergence.
